topic: Household Expense Tracker — Personal to Shared
questionsAsked: Who are the primary users of this expense tracker?
What is the primary client for MVP?
How should expenses be entered for MVP?
What level of shared expense features for MVP?
constraints: Manual entry + CSV import for MVP

candidates.name: Household Ledger
candidates.summary: Double-entry household finance system. Income pools into the household, expenses draw from it. Abstracted from users behind simple add-expense UX. Firefly III-inspired ledger with household member roles. Phase 1 is a standalone solo tracker, Phase 2 adds household sharing.
risks: Double-entry complexity hidden from UI but present in data model — careful API abstraction needed
CSV import variance across banks — configurable column mapping required
Household permission edge cases — start with simple owner/member roles
mvp: Dashboard (balance, income vs spending, category breakdown, recent transactions)
Monthly budgets per category with progress

candidates.name: Flex Wallet
candidates.summary: Multi-wallet system with personal AND shared wallets. Each user maintains private wallets alongside household shared wallets. Monarch Money-inspired with visibility tiers. More flexible for couples who keep some finances separate.
risks: Wallet sprawl confuses UX — need smart defaults
Aggregated reporting across multiple wallets adds complexity
Privacy model edge cases with visibility tiers

candidates.name: Envelope Budget
candidates.summary: Visual envelope budgeting inspired by YNAB/Goodbudget. Income fills envelopes, spending drains them. Simpler mental model but significantly less extensible. No double-entry, no multi-account support.
risks: Cannot add multi-account or transfer tracking without a rewrite
Limited audit trail compared to double-entry
Dead-end architecture for future features (bank sync, investments)

recommendation.reasoning: Double-entry is the right foundation — abstracted from users but gives consistent reporting, balance tracking, and extensibility. Pooled income is native to this model. Phase 1 ships as a useful solo tracker. Flex Wallet is the backup if users need separate finances alongside shared ones — can be layered on top of the ledger later.
